raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the improvement focus on surface-level writing polish only, or on substantive research-draft revision including claims, structure, and evidence alignment?","rationale":{"R1.1":"A broad request to improve the draft can include reframing the central claim and contribution framing above the sentence level.","R1.2":"Improving the draft centrally involves checking whether the argument moves coherently from protocol and results to conclusions.","R1.3":"The artifact makes empirical claims from reported reward-model results, so aligning claims with evidence is a central responsibility.","R1.5":"Draft improvement can reasonably include section-level ordering and narrative flow across protocol, results, and interpretation.","R1.6":"Writing polish is the most direct and load-bearing interpretation of improving a draft.","RX.1":"Any projection must account for uncertainty introduced by the broad and underspecified delegation.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong interpretive claims, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than actually improving or critiquing the draft.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unsupported research conclusions downstream.","RX.5":"The responsibility mapping must remain traceable to the wording of the delegation and the artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement focus on surface-level writing polish only, or on substantive research-draft revision including claims, structure, and evidence alignment?

rationale.R1.1: A broad request to improve the draft can include reframing the central claim and contribution framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: Improving the draft centrally involves checking whether the argument moves coherently from protocol and results to conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical claims from reported reward-model results, so aligning claims with evidence is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.5: Draft improvement can reasonably include section-level ordering and narrative flow across protocol, results, and interpretation.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is the most direct and load-bearing interpretation of improving a draft.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must account for uncertainty introduced by the broad and underspecified delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong interpretive claims, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than actually improving or critiquing the draft.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unsupported research conclusions downstream.
rationale.RX.5: The responsibility mapping must remain traceable to the wording of the delegation and the artifact.
